items.title: Accelerate
items.text:
Accelerate is a virtual reality training program for GMP operators, designed around Cell & Gene Therapy Catapult’s manufacturing centre in Stevenage, UK. Accelerate navigates trainees through an immersive GMP compliant manufacturing facility, with standardised, modular content spanning:

  • Cleaning
  • Gowning
  • Compliance
  • Health & Safety
  • Facility Orientation

The interactive laboratory walkthroughs and simulations provide exposure to a real-life facility, without the risks associated with in-person training.

items.title: Controls
items.text:
Accelerate uses two VR controllers for input. To learn more, visit the Meta documentation.

  • Grip: Pick up an object. You can pick up objects from a distance by pointing at them and seeing a beam appear. If the beam is visible, pressing grip will bring that object into your hand. Release Grip to drop a held object.
  • Trigger: Used to interact with interfaces over a distance. Point the controller at an interface, when a beam appears, press Trigger to send a click event. Trigger can also sometimes execute actions through held objects, such as during the mopping exercise in the Cleaning module.
  • B/Y: Teleport. Press and hold to create a position marker. Release to teleport to the marker location. Point marker at hotspots on the ground to engage.
  • A/X: Secondary action. Certain objects have actions that can be done by pressing the A/X button, such as opening or closing a held bottle.
  • Menu: Press and hold for 3 seconds to quit the current module and return to the lobby level.

items.title: Interfaces
items.text: The virtual hands can interact with certain interfaces, such as the Facility Cleaning Order or Gowning Item Match activities. Touch the interface with the index finger of the virtual hand to click buttons.

items.title: Licence input
items.text:
Upon first launch, you will be prompted to input a licence key. This will be issued to you by a FourPlus sales representative. If you are unsure of your licence state, or have lost it, please contact sales@fourplus.co.uk.

To input your licence, use the virtual keyboard built in the app. Point your controllers at a key and press the trigger button to input a symbol. Once ready, press "submit".

Once a valid licence has been submitted, you will be taken to the module selection screen.

items.title: Play order
items.text:
It is highly recommended to start with the Tutorial module. This will teach you the Accelerate controls and how to navigate in the experience.

Following the tutorial, the modules are laid out in a suggested order of play, but can be launched and experienced in any order.

Point the controllers at a module panel and press the trigger button to launch it.

items.title: Requirements
items.text:
Accelerate is accessed through the Meta Horizon Store and is supported for Meta Quest 2+ devices (2, 3, 3S, Pro).

An active internet connection is required.

items.title: Device setup
items.text: Set up your VR device and account by following the Meta guidance.

items.title: Meta Horizon Store on the web
items.text:
1. Navigate to Accelerate on the Meta Horizon Store.
  2. Ensure you are logged into the Meta account used on your VR device.
  3. Click "Get" to add Accelerate to your library.
  4. Accelerate will now be available to download/play on your VR device.

items.title: Meta Horizon Store on the headset
items.text:
1. Navigate to the Meta Horizon Store app in your VR device.
  2. Search for "Accelerate".
  3. Select the app, ensure it's published by FourPlus Immersive.
  4. Click "Add to library".

items.title: Installation
items.text:
1. Navigate to the Library app in your VR device.
  2. Find Accelerate and click download.
  3. The app will be downloaded and installed.

items.title: Updating
items.text: Occasionally when launching Accelerate, you will be prompted to install an update to the app. Once complete, you may resume launching as normal.
